rulings.Case Title: CATHERAL HILL TOWER CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION VS. LARISA GARBAR et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 12, 2007, LINE 2. PLAINTIFF CATHEDRAL HILL TOWER CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION BASED ON CIVIL CODE SECTION 3422. ARTICLE 3 E OF C.C.& R.'S ADDRESSES RESTRICTIONS ON THE USE OF BALCONIES, INCLUDING PLACING THINGS ON THE BALCONY. THE ASSOCIATIONS' REQUEST TO ENJOIN DEFENDANT FROM PLACING TILE ON THE BALCONY IS REASONABLE UNDER LAMDEN V LA JOLLA SHORES CLUBDOMINIUM (1999) 21 CAL 4TH 249. IT ALSO IS REASONABLE UNDER ARTICLE 2 SECTION 2F AS RELATED TO THE NEED TO MAINTAIN THE BUILDING. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: CATHERAL HILL TOWER CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION VS. LARISA GARBAR et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, DECEMBER 12, 2007, LINE 2. DEFENDANT LARISA GARBAR'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR THE REASONS STATED IN CONNECTION WITH PLAINTIFF'S MOTION. DENY AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION BECAUSE THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WAIVER AND LACHES. =(302/PJM/PB)
